source_test: HEC.FDA.ModelTest/unittests/PerformanceTest.cs + DefaultThresholdShould.cs exercise PerformanceByThresholds.GetThreshold/ListOfThresholds against a real ImpactAreaScenarioSimulation; no upstream unit test builds a bare PerformanceByThresholds directly, so this fixture is planning-captured at the Threshold/PerformanceByThresholds level, modeled on those call sites (GetThreshold(id).SystemPerformanceResults.<metric>()).
note: One case: constructs a PerformanceByThresholds, add_threshold's a DefaultExteriorStage (id 1) and an AdditionalExteriorStage (id 7) Threshold (distinct ids/values, both built via the (id, ConvergenceCriteria, ThresholdEnum, value) ctor), then get_threshold(get_threshold_id) retrieves the AdditionalExteriorStage one. threshold_value/threshold_type/threshold_id assert straight off the retrieved Threshold's own ctor-assigned fields (plain data echoes, not oracle-computed -- exact literals, not "PIN"). mean_aep exercises the round trip: aep_observations ({iteration,result}) are fed into the retrieved threshold's held SystemPerformanceResults via add_aep_for_assurance, one put_data_into_histograms() call, then mean_aep() proves the sub-object reached through add_threshold+get_threshold is the SAME live object the ctor built (not a copy) and that its math is wired -- pinned from the real C# via the oracle gate. Matches EvalPerformanceByThresholds in Program.cs and run_performance_by_thresholds in test_fixtures.cpp case-for-case.
